auc: 0.80 (FFDM aided radiologists); 0.79 (FFDM standalone); 0.83 (DBT aided radiologists); 0.84 (DBT standalone)
intended_use: MammoScreen is intended for use as a concurrent reading aid for interpreting physicians, to help identify findings on screening FFDM or DBT acquired with compatible mammography systems, and assess the
intended_use_full: MammoScreen is intended for use as a concurrent reading aid for interpreting physicians, to help identify findings on screening FFDM or DBT acquired with compatible mammography systems, and assess their level of suspicion. Output of the device includes marks placed on findings on the mammogram and level of suspicion scores. The findings could be soft tissue lesions or calcifications. The level of suspicion score is expressed at the finding level, for each breast and overall for the mammogram. Patient management decisions should not be made solely on the basis of analysis by MammoScreen.
target_condition: breast cancer detection in screening mammography
ground_truth: Biopsy-proven examples of breast cancer and normal tissue used for algorithm training; reader studies evaluated by AUC assessment
other_metrics: Radiologist performance improvement measured by AUC change from 0.77 to 0.80 for FFDM and 0.79 to 0.83 for DBT when using MammoScreen
